# Users per binary (process exec anomaly)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Process-execution frequency / anomaly hunting — surface noisy or rare binaries per host. Users per binary. `count_distinct()` is the distinct-count (never `dcount`); `if(c, a, b)` classifies inline (nest for multi-branch; `case` does not exist).

```sql
dataset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name != null | alter bin_lc = lowercase(actor_process_image_name) | comp count_distinct(actor_effective_username) as distinct_users, count() as execs by bin_lc | filter distinct_users > 0 | alter shared = if(distinct_users > 5, "widely-shared", "narrow") | sort desc distinct_users | limit 35
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (returned 17). From the 20-scenario XQL coverage program; technique is dataset-portable._
